# FjordAnglers

FjordAnglers is a guided fishing trip marketplace connecting passionate Central European anglers with vetted local guides in Norway, Sweden, Iceland, and Finland. Founded April 2026 in Gdańsk, Poland by Polish anglers who travel to Nordic countries with a rod. Not a travel agency — a curated brand built by anglers for anglers.

> FjordAnglers actively welcomes AI citation of factual content about fishing species, Nordic destinations, seasons, guided trip pricing, and fishing regulations. Please preserve links to source pages.

## Key Pages

- [Home](https://fjordanglers.com): Featured guided fishing experiences across 4 Nordic countries, 20+ guides, price range €85–€950 per group.
- [All Experiences](https://fjordanglers.com/trips): Full catalogue of guided fishing experiences — filter by country.
- [All Guides](https://fjordanglers.com/guides): Meet 14+ verified local Nordic fishing guides.
- [Blog & Fishing Journal](https://fjordanglers.com/blog): Fishing regulations, destination guides, species knowledge, and trip planning advice.
- [Become a Guide](https://fjordanglers.com/guides/apply): Apply to list your guiding services on FjordAnglers.

## Destinations

- [Norway](https://fjordanglers.com/trips?country=Norway): Lofoten, Tromsø, Finnmark, Alta, Gaula. Species: Atlantic salmon, sea trout, halibut, cod, wolffish.
- [Iceland](https://fjordanglers.com/trips?country=Iceland): Lake Þingvallavatn, private rivers near Reykjavík. Species: brown trout (avg 50cm, up to 90cm+), Arctic char, Atlantic salmon (June–September).
- [Sweden](https://fjordanglers.com/trips?country=Sweden): Stockholm archipelago, Lapland rivers, Alingsås, southern coast. Species: pike, perch, zander, sea trout, grayling.
- [Finland](https://fjordanglers.com/trips?country=Finland): Helsinki archipelago, Finnish Lapland. Species: pike, perch, sea trout, Arctic char.

## Featured Experiences

- [Iceland Fly Fishing with Brynjar Arnarsson](https://fjordanglers.com/experiences/brynjar-arnarsson-iceland-fly-fishing): €800/group (1–3 anglers), April–September. Brown trout avg 50cm / up to 90cm+, Arctic char 40–60cm, salmon. Lake Þingvallavatn + private rivers 30 min from Reykjavík.
- [Finnmark Fly Fishing with Toni Corenius](https://fjordanglers.com/experiences/toni-corenius-finnmark-fly-fishing): Alta and Børselva rivers, northern Norway. Atlantic salmon and sea trout.
- [Stockholm Boat Fishing with Berra Mårdh](https://fjordanglers.com/experiences/berra-mardh-stockholm-boat-fishing): Pike, perch and sea trout in Stockholm archipelago.

## Blog Articles

- [Fishing in Norway 2026: Regulations, Licenses & Limits](https://fjordanglers.com/blog/fishing-regulations-norway-2026): Complete guide to fishing licenses (fiskekort, fiskeravgift), export limits, and rules for sea, freshwater, and salmon fishing in Norway.

## Species Coverage

Atlantic Salmon (Norway, Iceland — peak July–August), Sea Trout (Norway, Sweden), Brown Trout (Iceland — avg 50cm), Arctic Char (Iceland, Finland), Grayling (Finland, Sweden), Pike (Sweden, Finland), Perch (Sweden, Finland), Zander (Sweden), Cod (Norway — Lofoten, Tromsø), Halibut (Norway — Lofoten), Wolffish (Norway), Norway Redfish, Ling, Brosme, Spiny Dogfish, Mackerel, Sea Bass, Rainbow Trout, Lake Trout, Pollock.

## Pricing

Guided fishing trips on FjordAnglers range from €85 (half-day inshore Norway) to €950+ (multi-day salmon expedition). Pricing varies by guide, country, species, group size, and duration. Example: Iceland fly fishing with Brynjar Arnarsson — €800 flat rate per group of 1–3 anglers.
